title: redb, PostgreSQL+RuVector, and nu_plugin CodeDB relationship decided
owner: David / LifeOS Data Agent / Runtime Kernel
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
decision_summary: Adopt the Architecture Blueprint's intended tiered memory hierarchy as the target authority model: PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical owner of durable relational + semantic macro-state; redb is the transient high-frequency tier (buffer / scratchpad / write-ahead log / embedding dedup cache / geometry engine); AgentDB (.rvf) is per-agent non-authoritative active cognition; envctl is the one-directional bridge (redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL) and the projection engine (PostgreSQL -> filesystem); nu_plugin CodeDB is the parser/ingestion path that buffers parsed AST blocks into redb. CodeDB's present durable-on-redb source-blob store is a TRANSITIONAL implementation, not the target; it migrates to PostgreSQL-canonical per data class via the already-implemented BlobStore SHA-256 content-addressed parity contract, one verified swap at a time.

correction_of_prior_brief.prior_recommendation: corrected layered hybrid that treats current durable-redb usage as a capability to preserve indefinitely, labeling any redb->transient reclassification a downgrade forbidden by PGAUTH-002
correction_of_prior_brief.why_it_was_a_mistake: The prior brief inverted the intended authority direction. The Architecture Blueprint (foundational source of truth) is explicit and repeated: redb's INTENDED role is the transient buffer / local AI scratchpad / application-level WAL / dedup cache / geometry engine (process-lifecycle, local-disk persistence), and PostgreSQL + RuVector is the INTENDED canonical durable owner (enterprise ACID / PITR, relational + knowledge-graph). The Architecture Context Matrix classifies PostgreSQL+RuVector as 'Canonical state' and redb as 'Temporary/derived state'. CodeDB's current durable-on-redb blobs are a transitional artifact of an incomplete build, not the target authority. Building toward the intended spec (redb -> transient, PostgreSQL -> canonical) is completing the design, i.e. an UPGRADE, never a downgrade. Owner directive: 'Building to intended specs is never a downgrade.'
correction_of_prior_brief.downgrade_protection_reframed: Upgrades-not-downgrades is honored by the MIGRATION MECHANISM, not by freezing redb as durable. No durable data is lost because cutover uses the existing BlobStore parity contract (identical SHA-256 identity across backends); a class only moves off redb-durable AFTER its PostgreSQL replacement is verified green. The guardrail is 'no unverified cutover', not 'redb stays canonical'.

state_class_assignments.class: durable relational macro-state (identities, histories, permissions, provenance, module_path metadata, code AST/rows, causal dependency edges)
state_class_assignments.role_of_others: redb may hold a transient forward buffer; envctl is the writer bridge
state_class_assignments.migration_path: codedb_store_pg::PgStore already implements the BlobStore contract; blobstore_parity.rs proves symmetry; cutover per class is a verified swap under one SHA-256 identity

state_class_assignments.class: semantic / vector / knowledge-graph state (embeddings, hybrid search indexes, GNN causal edges)
state_class_assignments.role_of_others: redb performs f32 distance math only as a cache; it has no SQL / secondary index / native vector logic (REDB-CLAIM-008/009/010 refuted, 013 qualified)
state_class_assignments.sync_direction: embeddings computed by a local model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) during envctl bridge, stored in PostgreSQL
state_class_assignments.migration_path: RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation must be proven locally before this class fully cuts over; until then embeddings may be produced via the proven Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path and committed to PostgreSQL

state_class_assignments.class: transient high-frequency state (agent thoughts, terminal traces, intermediate logs, state flags, raw-text BLAKE3 dedup hashes, WAL during PostgreSQL latency/outage)
state_class_assignments.role_of_others: PostgreSQL receives only settled/significant states flushed forward by envctl
state_class_assignments.sync_direction: redb -> envctl flush -> PostgreSQL (only significant states persist)
state_class_assignments.recovery_source: redb local disk (process-lifecycle); on loss, state is transient by definition
state_class_assignments.migration_path: none required; this is redb's intended terminal role

state_class_assignments.class: per-agent cognition (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy matrices, reinforcement-learning weights)
state_class_assignments.role_of_others: non-authoritative for shared macro-state; witnessed by Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors
state_class_assignments.sync_direction: self-contained; provenance links back to PostgreSQL knowledge graph
state_class_assignments.recovery_source: AgentDB .rvf file; regenerable from base model + training history
state_class_assignments.migration_path: none; AgentDB is the intended owner of active cognition

state_class_assignments.class: derived projections (materialized source directories for musl compilation, exported release trees)
state_class_assignments.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL (source of truth) projected by envctl / CodeDB materialization
state_class_assignments.role_of_others: filesystem projections are derived and disposable; regenerated deterministically from PostgreSQL rows via module_path metadata
state_class_assignments.migration_path: none; projection is the intended read path

state_class_assignments.class: external-secret / provenance authority (API keys, attestation bundles, source pins)
state_class_assignments.role_of_others: PostgreSQL stores references/provenance, never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets

invariants: Single durable writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class.
Forward-only sync: redb -> PostgreSQL via envctl; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write.
Verified cutover: a class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL for that class; never by assumption.
No sidecar: RuVector runs as a native PostgreSQL extension; no separate vector database in the target.
Provenance preserved: every migrated class keeps its SHA-256 identity and its source commit/PR lineage (adopted_capabilities.source.csv).
open_dependencies: Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation not yet locally proven; semantic-class full cutover waits on it (NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder).
Live blobstore_parity.rs green run against disposable PostgreSQL captures the durable-class cutover proof (CDB086 / CDB106-AC05 lane; PostgreSQL provisioned this run).
